title: Accounts Assistant
description: **Your new company** Your new role is with an impressive and inspiring not\-for\-profit organisation based in Dublin City Centre. You will be part of a busy finance team and reporting directly to the finance manager. The role is hybrid working with flexible start times. The role is a 12 month contract with the potential for it to become permanent. **Your new role** In your new role you will be looking after the full finance function as a senior accounts assistant. You will be responsible for the creditors and debtors ledger, assisting with preparation of month and year\-end, bank reconciliations, payment runs and VAT returns. You will play an integral role in the day to day finance and assist on upcoming projects where required. **What you'll need to succeed** To succeed you will need to have over 4 years experience as a senior accounts assistant or bookkeeper. You will also be Part Qualified or have an ATI qualification. You will have had experience in a busy finance team and work well under pressure. Previous experience in a Not For Profit finance team is beneficial but not essential. You will have great attention to detail and be a team player. You will also have good accounting software experience and be proficient in Excel. **What you'll get in return** In return, you will receive a salary of up to €45,000\. The company has hybrid working and a flexible start time. This role is an initial a 12 month contract with the view to becoming permanent. Once permanent, there are numerous benefits contributions, including healthcare, pension contribution and bonus scheme. **What you need to do now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career. **Desired Skills and Experience** To succeed you will need to have over 4 years experience as a senior accounts assistant or bookkeeper. You will also be Part Qualified or have an ATI qualification. You will have had experience in a busy finance team and work well under pressure. Previous experience in a Not For Profit finance team is beneficial but not essential. You will have great attention to detail and be a team player. You will also have good accounting software experience and be proficient in Excel. title: Advisory Investment Strategist
description: 25 May 2026 Dublin, IE, IE **About Goodbody** Goodbody is a leading financial services firm, committed to serving and growing client relationships for 150 years. We are a full service, investment\-led business offering wealth management, asset management, investment banking and private equity services. Our staff of over 400 work in offices across Dublin, London, Cork and Galway. We’re proud to offer prospective employees a professional, collegial work environment that encourages diversity of thought and background. Our reputation in the Irish market for putting customers first is as consistent as the company’s long\-standing commitment to giving back to the communities that we work in. **About the Advisory Investment Strategist Role:** The Advisory Investment Strategist plays a pivotal role within Goodbody Wealth Management’s Investment Strategy team, leading the firm’s Advisory investment proposition and generating high‑quality advice and outcomes for clients. This is a permanent, high‑impact specialist role that combines deep financial market and investment strategy knowledge with a high degree of stakeholder interaction, content generation, and business development. The role contributes directly to the growth of our business, delivering advisory investment solutions to relationship managers and strategic advisors, empowering them to provide our investor clients with clear, consistent and compliant advice to meet their investment objectives. The Advisory Investment Strategist will report directly to the Head of Investment Strategy and will play a pivotal role in the evolution of Goodbody’s Advisory investment offering. Subject to experience and qualification, the Advisory Investment Strategist would be expected to participate as a voting member of the firm’s Investment Committee. **Advisory Investment Strategist Accountabilities include:** **Content Creation** The Advisory Investment Strategist will be responsible for the delivery of day\-to\-day and week\-to\-week market intelligence and content, adapting our existing investment process to motivate relationship managers and engage the Advisory client base. Written and verbal communication of Advisory investment ideas, trends and themes are among the key scalable deliverables. Proactive motivation and responsive communication should drive high levels of engagement with stakeholders. **Product Innovation** Working closely with the idea generation and content provided by Goodbody Wealth Management’s Chief Investment Officer, asset class specialists, investment selection and investment solutions teams, the Advisory Investment Strategist will seek new and novel products and strategies to best express house views and themes in the Advisory context. Advisory solutions are based on reference model portfolios, using suitable instruments, from direct equities, exchange traded funds and mutual funds, to structured funds and products, private assets, and credit derivatives. The role will also participate in related business development projects. **Advisory Solutions** The role will be deeply involved in creating and presenting suitable solutions for Advisory clients, primarily high and ultra\-high net worth individuals and family advisory, working closely together with Goodbody relationship managers to identify the most attractive strategies to help investors reach their goals while adhering to highest standards of portfolio construction, asset allocation and risk management, with ongoing advice through the investment life cycle. The Advisory Investment Strategist will also benefit from high levels of dynamic feedback and idea generation from interaction with relationship managers and Advisory client investors. **Regulatory \& Technical Excellence** Applying deep technical product knowledge, regulatory awareness and high ethical and professional standards in all interactions, consistent with Goodbody’s values and regulatory obligations, the Advisory Investment Strategist will contribute to the firm’s provision of investment advice with the highest standards of technical accuracy, governance and documentation requirements. Playing a central role in the Advisory offering, the role will also help to shape the governance, standards, and consistency of our Advisory operating model, including development of how our advice is delivered, both digitally and personally, across platforms and to our client base. **Qualifications and Experience** Professional \& Academic Requirements The successful candidate will hold relevant third‑level and professional qualifications. The following are considered particularly suitable: * Financial or investment‑related third‑level qualification, ideally to Master’s level or above. * Chartered Financial Adviser (CFA) or Chartered Alternative Investments Adviser (CAIA) qualification. Experience The successful candidate will be an experienced financial market professional. The following are considered particularly suitable: * 5\+ years of experience in an investment strategy or related role, including portfolio construction, product innovation, and stakeholder communication. * Strong digital literacy including advanced use of MS Excel with VBA, Bloomberg including PORT, automation, practical professional experience with coding in Python. **Ideal Characteristics** We are seeking an ambitious, motivated professional who demonstrates: * An innovative and solution\-oriented mindset, capable of challenging existing approaches and contributing new perspectives. * Exceptional communication and presentation skills, explaining complex ideas in simple terms. * Excellent statistical and mathematical skills, analytical ability, and impeccable attention to detail. * Proven ability to deliver tangible outcomes through personal initiative and technical excellence. * A strong team orientation, thriving in a dynamic environment with multiple stakeholders and evolving client needs. * Strong relationship‑building capability across multidisciplinary teams. * Ability to build and manage a consistent, repeatable analytical framework to generate opportunities and engage confidently with professional advisers and introducers. * Commitment to the highest ethical and professional standards in client work and internal collaboration. **Employee Value Proposition** Goodbody is focused on helping our employees thrive, and we do this by bringing to life our Employee Value Proposition (EVP) ‘Together we prosper’. Our firm’s purpose is to create long\-term prosperity, and we cannot execute on our purpose without the right talent at Goodbody. To make sure we attract and retain industry\-leading, passionate and inspiring talent, we’ve built a vibrant culture based on inclusion, opportunities to grow and a commitment to ‘do the right thing’, one of our core values. We live this by offering: * Regular employee events through our universal inclusion programme fostering a sense of belonging and celebrating differences. And an ‘always on’ approach to social, financial, mental and physical wellbeing. * A benefits package that includes a generous pension scheme, educational sponsorship and health insurance, along with a hybrid working model to promote better work/life balance * Opportunities to give back through sustainability initiatives, volunteering and on\-site and online meetings with our charity partners Goodbody is an equal opportunities employer and is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace which values and benefits from the diversity of our workforce. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process. Should you have a reasonable accommodation request please include a note in your application or email us directly at HR@Goodbody.ie Please read the Goodbody Recruitment Privacy Notice. By submitting your personal data to us, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this Privacy Notice and agree to the use of your personal data in line with this. We will only use the information that we collect about you lawfully in accordance with the Data Protection Acts, 1988 and 2003 and the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. **Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to Goodbody by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role.** Goodbody operates a direct sourcing model and where agency assistance is required, the HR team will engage directly with our recruitment partners. Goodbody Stockbrokers UC, trading as Goodbody, is regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland. In the UK, Goodbody is also subject to regulation by the Financial Conduct Authority. Goodbody is a member of Euronext Dublin and the London Stock Exchange. Goodbody is a member of the group of companies headed by AIB Group plc. For AIB Employees: Please note there are specific controls in place for transfers between AIB and Goodbody. Goodbody is part of AIB Group and you must inform your AIB line manager and HR BP in advance of making an application to Goodbody. **Job Segment:** Construction Line Supervisor, Business Development, Investment Banking, Private Equity, Risk Management, Engineering, Sales, Finance

title: Android Developer
description: **Role: Android Payments Developer** **Location: Dublin, Ireland** **Type: Contract** **Job Description:** Senior Android Payments Developer — POS Legacy Transformation We are seeking a Senior Android Payments Developer to lead development of card acceptance applications on POS devices and play a significant role in a legacy modernisation programme. You will design and implement robust, secure Android software for terminals (including PAX devices), work closely with QA, product and programme teams, and drive payment certification with major card schemes, **Key Responsibilities:** Design and implement Android applications for point\-of sale terminals: deliver clean, maintainable Java/Kotlin code that runs on embedded Android and AOSP\-based POS devices. Integrate with POS hardware: implement and validate drivers and SDK integrations for card readers, PIN pads, NFC/contactless, EMM readers, printers and peripherals (experience with PAX SKs and device\-specific APIs expected). Payment flow implementation: authorisation, settlement, reversal and offline\-capable flows; ensure correct handling of EMV, contactless and magnetic stripe scenarios. Support payment certification: prepare artefacts, run test suites and coordinate certification activities with card schemes and acquirers (Visa, Mastercard, American Express and others); address scheme audit findings and remediation. Contribute to legacy transformation: migrate and refactor legacy payment codebases to modern architectures (modularisation, services, secure SDKS), preserving backward compatibility and minimising customer impact. Security and compliance: Implement secure coding practices, support PCI and terminal security requirements (PCI PTS, EMV kernel considerations), and liaise with security and HSM teams on key management interraces. Performance, reliability and observability: Profile and optimise application performance, improve startup times and memory usage, and add telemetry/diagnostics to aid support and SRE teams. Automation and C/CD: Build and maintain automated build, test and release pipelines for terminal images and application packages; support automated regression and device farm test runs. Defect resolution and production support: drive root cause analysis for critical incidents, produce fixes and participate in postrelease reviews. Mentoring and collaboration: review peers' code, share knowledge across the team, and work closely with QA leads, programme managers and product owners to align delivery priorities. Primary Objectives: Deliver high\-quality Android POS applications for multiple device families (including PAX) that meet functional, security and performance targets. Successfully completed payment certification cycles with major card schemes and acquirers, achieving approval with minimal rework. Advance the legacy transformation programme by migrating key components to modern, maintainable implementations while minimising disruption to services. Reduce production defects and incident mean time to resolution through better testing, telemetry and automated checks. Ensure software fulfils compliance requirements and is supportable in live environments. **Knowledge Skills:** Strong hands\-on Android development experience (lava and Kotlin) on embedded/AOSP variants used in POS devices. Proven experience with POS device SKs and hardware integration —experience with PAX devices and SDKs highly desirable. Deep understanding of payment terminal flows, EV transaction lifecycle, contactless/NFC processing and implications for application logic. Practical familiarity with payment scheme certification processes and requirements for Visa, Mastercard, American Express (preparing logs, test cases, remediation). Awareness of industry security standards: PCI (including PCI PTS) and EMV kernel behaviours; understanding of secure key management concepts and HSM interactions. Experience with automated testing and Cl/CD for mobile/embedded applications; unit, integration, Ul automation and device farm orchestration. Proficient with modern development toolchains: Android Studio, Gradle, Git, experience with Jenkins/GitLab Cl/Azure DevOps or equivalent. Competence in debugging on physical devices, serial/USB/Bluetooth/NFC communications, and analysing logs and traces from constrained environments. Strong analytical skills, attention to detail and methodical approach to debugging and problem resolution. Effective verbal and written communication; able to prepare technical artefacts for certification, and present technical trade\-offs to product and programme stakeholders. Experience working in regulated/financial environments (fintech, payments) and in large enterprise delivery contexts. **Educational Professional Requirements:** Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering or equivalent practical experience. Relevant certifications (Android, EMV, PCI or equivalent) are advantageous. Demonstrable track record of shipping payment terminal software and participating in card\-scheme certification or acquirer testing is highly desirable

title: Apprentice Cash Services Operative
description: We are recruiting for Drivers in our Dublin Branch This is an excellent opportunity to join an established team working as a key resource for an essential service, distributing cash through society in a secure and safe manner. **Main Purpose Of Job** We require full time CIT Operative under The Transport Operations \& Commercial Driving Apprenticeship Programme (CDAP) in our dublin branch . This role is to carry out the collection and delivery of cash and coin to and from customer premises. Replenishing of ATM’s and providing maintenance. Perform various tasks assigned by the Manager/ Supervisor. While also attending college 1 day per week for studies. **Key Skills And Attributes** * Previous experience in a customer service environment. * B License (2 years) required * The ability to work under pressure and to dead\-lines. * Strong communication skills with the ability to carry out tasks as assigned. * Good knowledge of surrounding areas. * A Private Security License would be an advantage. All applicants must reside in Ireland \& commit to doing a driving assesment on a van **Skills** Garda Vetted Flexibility Required Drivers

title: Apprentice Lift Engineer
description: Byrne Lifts is an Irish\-owned, Guaranteed Irish lift specialist with over 40 years of experience delivering lift installation, modernisation, maintenance, and repair services across Ireland. We work with leading facilities management companies, property managers, developers, healthcare organisations, hotels, and building owners nationwide. We are looking for a motivated and enthusiastic **Apprentice Lift Engineer** to join our growing team. This is an exciting opportunity for someone who enjoys practical work, problem solving, and technology, and wants to build a long\-term career within a growing engineering business. Working alongside experienced Lift Engineers, you will receive hands\-on training and practical experience across all areas of the business while developing the skills required to become a qualified Lift Engineer. As part of the apprenticeship programme, the successful candidate will also undertake a structured **Electrician Trade Apprenticeship and training programme**, combining classroom\-based learning with practical site experience to develop the electrical and technical skills required within the lift industry. **What You Will Learn** You will gain practical experience in: * Lift installation and commissioning * Preventative maintenance and servicing * Lift modernisation and upgrade projects * Fault finding and diagnostics * Mechanical and electrical systems * Lift control systems and safety devices * Reading technical drawings and documentation * Health \& Safety procedures and safe working practices * Customer communication and site interaction * Digital reporting systems and work management software * Complete all apprenticeship coursework, training and exam requirements **Candidate Requirements** We are looking for someone who: * Has a strong interest in engineering, mechanics, electrical systems or construction * Enjoys practical hands\-on work * Has good problem\-solving skills * Has good communication and interpersonal skills * Is motivated and willing to learn * Can work as part of a team * Demonstrates attention to detail * Has a positive attitude and strong work ethic * Is safety conscious * Is flexible and willing to travel when required **Qualifications / Experience** The following would be an advantage but are not essential: * Leaving Certificate or equivalent * Electrical or trade experience (part qualified electrician) * Full driving licence (or working towards one) To apply, please send your CV and a short cover note outlining your interest in engineering and why you would like to build your career with Byrne Lifts. Work Location: In person

title: Associate DevSecOps Engineer - based in Luxembourg
description: **This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence. The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi\-cultural environment.** **We also offer relocation support.** The **EIB** , the European Union's bank, is seeking to recruit for its Group Information Systems Directorate (GIS), Core Platforms \& Resilience Department (CPR), Platform Engineering \& Operations Division (PEO), Cloud, DevSecOps \& SDLC Enablement Unit (CDSE) at its headquarters in Luxembourg, an **Associate DevSecOps Engineer** **\*.** **This is a full\-time position at grade 4 for which the EIB offers a permanent contract.** * internal benchmark Associate Engineer IT Technology \& Infrastructure *Panel interviews are foreseen as from June 2026* Purpose The Associate DevSecOps Engineer plays a key role in embedding security into every stage of the software delivery lifecycle. Working closely with experienced DevSecOps engineers as well as development, infrastructure and security teams. This role contributes to building and improving secure, automated and compliant delivery pipelines and platforms. This position offers the opportunity to drive the adoption of DevSecOps practices across teams and support resilient CI/CD pipelines and cloud environments. It also provides hands\-on exposure to vulnerability management, security monitoring and modern secure software delivery in a collaborative and evolving technology environment. Operating Network You will report to the Head of Cloud, DevSecOps \& SDLC Enablement Unit (CDSE) unit and will cooperate with other platform teams in the same department as well as application teams in other departments Units, transversal teams IN Group Information Systems Directorate (GIS). Accountabilities **DevSecOps Platform Support \& Operations** * Contribute to the operation and day‑to‑day support of the DevSecOps toolchain (e.g. CI/CD platforms, code repositories, artefact management, infrastructure‑as‑code tooling), ensuring availability, stability and secure configuration of platform components. * Assist in the maintenance of secure CI/CD pipeline templates and shared components, applying platform standards and security baselines defined by the unit. **SDLC Enablement \& Governance Support** * Support the rollout and adoption of the EIB SDLC framework, including the implementation of mandatory quality gates, automated controls, and evidence collection mechanisms across delivery pipelines. * Help delivery teams understand and apply SDLC requirements by providing practical guidance, examples, and tooling support, under the supervision of senior engineers. **Security Integration \& Automation** * Assist in embedding security controls into the DevOps lifecycle, including static and dynamic code analysis, dependency and artefact scanning, secrets management, and infrastructure security checks. * Support the monitoring and remediation of security findings identified through automated scans, working with delivery teams and senior DevSecOps engineers to prioritise and resolve issues. **Collaboration With Delivery Teams** * Act as a first‑line technical contact for GIS software delivery teams on DevSecOps and SDLC platform usage, escalating complex issues where appropriate. * Collaborate with developers, testers, platform engineers and security stakeholders to improve developer experience while maintaining compliance‑by‑design. * Participate in communication and change management activities as an integrated aspect of IT solutions, infrastructure or processes. **Continuous Improvement \& Knowledge Management** * Contribute to the continuous improvement of DevSecOps and SDLC practices by documenting lessons learned, updating technical documentation, and proposing incremental enhancements to tooling and processes. * Recommend tools, technologies, and incremental improvements to overall quality and security of products infrastructure. **Compliance, Risk \& Operational Discipline** * Apply established security, risk, and compliance requirements (including internal policies and regulatory obligations) when supporting platform components and delivery pipelines. * Ensure that operational activities are performed in line with change, incident and problem management processes, contributing to audit readiness and traceability. **Qualifications** * University degree (minimum an equivalent to a Bachelor) preferably in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering or a related field. * At least 3 years of relevant professional experience in IT engineering roles related to DevOps, systems administration, cloud operations, software delivery, or security engineering. * Basic hands‑on experience with software delivery pipelines (CI/CD) and operational environments, ideally supporting development or platform teams. * Foundational understanding of DevSecOps principles, including “shift‑left” security and automation of controls. * Introductory experience with cloud or virtualised environments (e.g. Azure, AWS, VMware, container platforms). * Awareness of infrastructure‑as‑code concepts and automated provisioning, even if mainly assisting or consuming existing modules. * Familiarity with containerisation concepts (e.g. Docker images, registries), at user level rather than platform design level. * Basic proficiency in scripting or automation languages (e.g. Python, Bash, PowerShell), sufficient to read, adapt and troubleshoot scripts. * Excellent knowledge of English and/or French (\*\*\*), with a good command of the other. (Knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage). Competencies **Find Out More About EIB Core Behavioural Skills Here** To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here *(\*\*\*) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre\-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre\-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of* *level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1\.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.* We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process due to a disability, neurodivergence, or a chronic health condition, please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will manage your request appropriately. By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation. *This is an open campaign to consecutively fill open positions. The campaign will remain open until position is filled. Applications will be reviewed in order of receipt.*

title: Associate Mechanical Engineer
description: **ABOUT US** EDC was established in 2003 and thanks to our quality\-driven approach to engineering and early\-adopter ethos we have grown during a period when most companies in this sector have struggled. We have continued to increase our workload and deliver quality, innovative engineering. As pioneers in the design of low\-energy, high\-performance buildings and systems, EDC fully supports the importance placed on sustainability and welcomes the challenges this presents. We are first BIM Level 2 Certified Consultancy in the UK and Ireland and deliver projects from our four offices in Ireland – Cork, Limerick, Galway \& Dublin, our UK office based in London and our Istanbul Office based in Turkey. EDC has also seen significant recent expansion overseas in the African Market. **OUR MISSION** Committed to delivering excellence in MEP \& sustainability services where we can add positive impact to the environment and our society. **OUR VISION** To be recognised as the UK and Ireland's leading experts in sustainable MEP engineering, known for excellence in delivery, innovation, and a commitment to a resilient, environmentally responsible future achieved by putting our people first and investing in their continuous development. **THE ROLE \& RESPONSIBILITIES** For this role, we are looking for an Associate Mechanical Engineer who can lead change and mentor engineers within their team ensuring best\-in\-class engineering. As an a Associate Mechanical Engineer with EDC, you will be expected to have a thorough understanding of the theoretical and practical application of their engineering discipline, and a strong understanding of the other disciplines offered by the company while also leading and managing the full Electrical Design on projects of all sizes. * Lead the delivery of Mechanical and Electrical Engineering projects across all stages, taking full ownership of programme, quality, coordination, and overall project outcomes. * Undertake and oversee Mechanical design work as required, ensuring technical excellence from concept through to construction. * Manage the Mechanical and Electrical design process to ensure all deliverables meet project requirements, programme, and relevant standards. * Provide clear direction and leadership to project teams, including Senior Engineers and junior staff, ensuring accountability and effective performance across all project activities. * Manage project resources and support the planning and organisation of team workload to achieve efficient and timely delivery. * Take ownership of project cost control, maintaining awareness of budget, resource utilisation, and overall project performance, with responsibility for commercial and resource decisions on projects where appropriate. * Act as a key point of contact for clients, leading technical discussions, managing expectations, and supporting the development of strong and lasting client relationships. * Provide technical leadership and mentorship to junior engineers, supporting their development and progression, and contributing to capability building within the team. * Ensure quality control across Mechanical Engineering outputs and drive consistency of technical standards across projects. * Work collaboratively with multidisciplinary teams to ensure coordinated and efficient project delivery. * Drive the implementation and adoption of new systems, technologies, and processes where appropriate. * Assist BIM and modelling teams in producing coordinated 3D models and detailed engineering drawings. * Attend and lead design meetings, workshops, and site visits as required. * Travel to client and project locations where necessary to represent EDC professionally. * Maintain and promote the reputation of EDC within the industry and contribute to the development of client relationships, including supporting business development activities where required. * Collaborate with colleagues across EDC offices to support project delivery and business objectives. * Demonstrate ongoing development of leadership, commercial, and strategic capabilities in line with progression towards Associate Director. Follow EDC’s Environmental Management System (EMS), including all related policies, procedures, and training. Perform duties in a manner that supports the company’s environmental objectives and sustainability commitments. Work\-from\-home arrangements to be agreed with the line manager. The current expectation is office attendance three days per week, with flexibility to balance business and personal needs. **EDUCATION \& EXPERIENCE** * Minimum Degree level in Mechanical Engineering * 6 \+ years Building Services Consultancy Design experience. * Expert user of Microsoft Office * Experience with thermal modelling, energy assessment, dynamic energy modelling software advantageous. * Commercial sector knowledge and BIM experience a strong advantage. * Chartership Desirable. **KNOWLEDGE \& SKILLS** * Proven experience of delivering detailed designs through the full design cycle. * Expert knowledge and experience in the following Mechanical design areas: Water Services, Heating / LTHW, HVAC, Fuel, above\- ground drainage and BMS. * Deep knowledge of building regulations, particularly TGD part B, F, G, L and the ability to demonstrate this knowledge in design and project lead. * Excel at working on several projects simultaneously and show the ability to prioritise and plan work. * Ability to lead projects internally and externally and be responsible for project performances, including satisfied clients, profitable projects, and maintaining EDC’s reputation to enable repeat work. * Demonstrate a good understanding of other building disciplines in the design and installation of buildings. * Exhibit leadership qualities while working as part of a team. * Ability to make prompt, disciplined project decisions. * Excellent attention to detail and interpersonal skills. * Commercial Awareness. **BENEFITS** * Competitive Salary * Paid Overtime / Time In Lieu / Flexitime * Performance \& Development Management, including Annual salary reviews and Annual Performance related Bonus Payment * Annual Wellbeing Payment * Company Matched Pension Scheme \& Death in Service * Industry Leading 28 Days Leave * Educational \& Masters Support * 1 extra week’s paid leave on reaching 5th Anniversary * Chartership Pathway \& Paid Membership Fees * Engineering Excellence Weekend (Weekend with Team EDC Team) \- Previous locations include Killarney, Edinburgh, Bantry, Brighton, Aran Islands. * Employee Assistance Programme * Annual Health Checks * Flexible \& Remote working all year round * 1 month continuous remote working from any location, once a year. (Work from anywhere for 1 month, once a year) * Highly accredited People Experience Promise (Engineering Excellence, Together) focusing on Winning, Growth, Innovation, Leadership and People Focus. * Bike to Work Scheme * Employee of the Quarter \& Year \- Financial reward and public recognition **OUR HIRING PROCESS** **We know you live a busy life and so, we have adopted a hiring process that is targeted and streamlined. We focus on your fit with EDC as well as your technical skill match.** **APPLY – REVIEW – INTERVIEW – OFFER \- ONBOARDING** **Apply:** Find an open role that interests you with EDC via one of our recruitment channels. **Review:** Applications are read by real engineering managers who are both experts in interpreting resumes and are familiar with all EDC’s jobs within a talent segment—not just the one you applied for. **Interview:** We use a competencies\-based interviewing process that is intended to help us minimize unconscious bias while also making sure we hire people who will thrive within EDC. **Offer:** We ensure we come back as quickly as possible with regards to whether you will be made an offer. If you verbally accept, we will send through an official written offer and begin the onboarding process.

title: Associate QC Microbiology Specialist
description: Orion Group Life Sciences are currently recruiting an Associate QC Microbiology Specialist on behalf of our Multinational Biopharmaceutical Client based in Carlow on an initial 11\-Month contract with potential to extend. Monday \- Friday role, 8:00\- 16:30 and will be fully onsite **Key Responsibilities** * Supporting the environmental control program by trend analysis and implementation of appropriate corrective and preventative measures. * Supporting and owner of Environmental monitoring risk assessments and qualification. * Generation of quarterly and annual trend reports. * Understanding and applying regulatory/compliance and compendial requirements to their role together with remaining current on upcoming regulatory and compliance changes. * Development and execution of Microbiology test methods and project validations. * Troubleshooting of issues which arise during the execution of validation studies and routine tests. * Development of training modules. * Microbiology Support to Site Investigations and projects relating to environmental monitoring and utilities monitoring. * Driving Continuous Improvement initiatives, including lean lab initiatives, method optimization/redevelopment. * Authoring and managing change controls, procedures, test methods and deviations. * Result authorization for microbiological samples to support batch release. * GLIMS System updates. * Assessing compendial and quality manual updates pertaining to the Microbiology Lab \& environmental control. * Supporting lab stock management and financial control as required. * Interpreting customer needs, assessing requirements, and identifying solutions to non\-standard requests. * Making decisions within guidelines and policies which impact microbiology projects. * Ensuring highest safety standards. * Performing other duties as assigned. **Qualifications** *Required* * Bachelor’s degree in Microbiology, Biology, Biotechnology, Pharmaceutical Science, or a related Life Sciences field. * Minimum 3 years of hands\-on experience in microbiology/QC, specifically in environmental monitoring and utilities monitoring, supporting site projects, trending, risk assessments, and data analysis. * Ability to author procedures, change controls, deviations, reports, and work in line with regulatory/compendial requirements. * Strong troubleshooting, decision\-making, and ability to drive improvements such as lean lab initiatives and method optimization. * Strong microbiology background and strong understanding of aseptic processing, environmental monitoring programs, and contamination control in sterile manufacturing. * Experience in a GMP QC laboratory with knowledge of regulatory expectations. * Familiarity with ALCOA(\+) data integrity principles, electronic laboratory systems, and controlled documentation. * Entry\- to early\-career level with growing technical competence; able to perform routine tasks with limited discretion. *Preferred* * Experience with problem solving, decision making, scheduling of laboratory testing/tasks **People** are our business **worldwide** Orion Group was founded in 1987 and is now one of the largest, independent, international recruitment companies. We have a network of 200 employees working from 24 offices, delivering a range of services – Talent Acquisition, Recruitment Outsourcing Services, Retained Search, Global Workforce Solutions, Completions \& Commissioning and Materials Management – across 68 countries. As a global leader in workforce solutions, we recruit personnel across the Engineering \& Technical, Office \& Commercial, Scientific and Skilled Trades disciplines, for sectors including Oil \& Gas, Life Science, Power \& Utilities, Constructions \& Infrastructure, Manufacturing and Renewables.

title: Associate Specialist Quality Control
description: ### **Job Information** Date Opened 05/25/2026 Job Type Full time Industry Pharma/Biotech/Clinical Research Work Experience 4\-5 years City Carlow State/Province Carlow Country Ireland ### **Job Description** **About PSC Biotech** **Who are we?** PSC Biotech is a leading Biotech Consultancy firm founded in 1996, headquartered in Pomona, California, USA, with Global operations in Ireland, India, Singapore, Australia and the US, serving 350 clients in more than 23 countries worldwide. We provide cloud\-based software solutions for Quality Management and Regulatory Inspections, pharmaceuticals contract manufacturing professionals, and metrology services to our clients. **‘Take your Career to a new Level’** PSC Biotech disrupts the conventional consultancy model by aligning our EVP as one of the unique selling point which includes the opportunity to work with the most talented cohort of like\-minded professionals operating in the Pharma/ Biotech Industry. We offer a permanent contract of employment giving exposure of working in Top Pharmaceutical client sites in a diverse\-cultural work setting. **Employee Value Proposition** Employees are the “heartbeat” of PSC Biotech, we provide unparalleled empowering career development though Learning \& Development in\-house training mentorship through constant guidance to facilitate career progression. We believe in creating high performing teams that can exceed our client’s expectations with regards to quality of all scalable and business unit deliverables, staying under budget and ensuring timelines for our deliverables are being met. **Overview:** We are seeking an Associate Specialist Quality Control to provide technical support to the QC Microbiology Lab \& operations to ensure the continued manufacture and supply of quality pharmaceutical products in meeting the priorities of: Compliance, Supply, Strategy and Profit Plan. trend analysis and implementation of appropriate corrective and preventative measures. * Supporting and owner of Environmental monitoring risk assessments and qualification. * Generation of quarterly and annual trend reports. * Understanding and applying regulatory/compliance and compendial requirements to their role together with remaining current on upcoming regulatory and compliance changes. * Development and execution of Microbiology test methods and project validations. * Troubleshooting of issues which arise during the execution of validation studies and routine tests. * Development of training modules. * Microbiology Support to Site Investigations and projects relating to environmental monitoring and utilities monitoring. * Driving Continuous Improvement initiatives, including lean lab initiatives, method optimization/redevelopment. * Authoring and managing change controls, procedures, test methods and deviations. * Result authorization for microbiological samples to support batch release. * GLIMS System updates. * Assessing compendial and quality manual updates pertaining to the Microbiology Lab \& environmental control. * Supporting lab stock management and financial control as required. * Interpreting customer needs, assessing requirements, and identifying solutions to non\-standard requests. * Making decisions within guidelines and policies which impact microbiology projects. * Ensuring highest safety standards. * Performing other duties as assigned. **Qualifications:** **Required** * Bachelor’s degree in Microbiology, Biology, Biotechnology, Pharmaceutical Science, or a related Life Sciences field. * Minimum 3 years of hands\-on experience in microbiology/QC, specifically in environmental monitoring and utilities monitoring, supporting site projects, trending, risk assessments, and data analysis. * Ability to author procedures, change controls, deviations, reports, and work in line with regulatory/compendial requirements. * Strong troubleshooting, decision\-making, and ability to drive improvements such as lean lab initiatives and method optimization. * Strong microbiology background and strong understanding of aseptic processing, environmental monitoring programs, and contamination control in sterile manufacturing. * Experience in a GMP QC laboratory with knowledge of regulatory expectations. * Familiarity with ALCOA(\+) data integrity principles, electronic laboratory systems, and controlled documentation. * Entry\- to early\-career level with growing technical competence; able to perform routine tasks with limited discretion. **Preferred:** * Experience with problem solving, decision making, scheduling of laboratory testing/tasks

title: Audit Graduate Programme 2026
description: **Join Crowe Ireland’s 2026 Audit Graduate Programme!** Are you ready to kickstart your career and write your story with one of Ireland's top accounting and advisory firms? At **Crowe Ireland** , we’re not just about numbers—we’re about people, innovation, and making a real impact. We’re advisors, partners, and innovators who help businesses grow, navigate their challenges, and build a brighter future. We work with a diverse mix of clients – from owner\-managed business and not\-for\-profit to multinationals, government agencies and hospitality as a small example – Every day brings something new! As part of our team, you'll learn from the best, gain hands\-on experience across a range of industries, and be part of a community that values **collaboration** , **curiosity** , and **creativity** . **Why choose Crowe Ireland?** At Crowe Ireland, we are a close\-knit team with a global reach. Our firm is all about fostering talent, encouraging fresh ideas, and driving positive change for our clients and community. We are big enough to offer great opportunities, international assignments and write a successful story and small enough for you to make great impacts on some of our projects from the word go. Join us, and you will be part of a dynamic and diverse team that values growth, learning and having a bit of fun along the way! If you want to know more about our Graduate Programme, visit our website . **What’s in it for You?** Our 2026 Audit \& Assurance Graduate Programme is designed to give you real\-world experience, professional development, and a clear pathway to becoming a qualified accountant. You’ll get to work with amazing clients, use trending technology, and learn from a team that genuinely cares about your success. Here’s what you’ll do: **Your Role:** * **Dive into Diverse Audits:** Get hands\-on experience working on a variety of audit assignments across different sectors—no two days are the same! * **Data Detective:** Use the latest tools and techniques to analyse client data, spot trends, and uncover insights that drive smart decisions. * **Business Insights:** Learn how businesses operate, identify areas for improvement, and build relationships with clients that go beyond the numbers. * **Impactful Reporting:** Draft high\-quality financial statements and audit reports, packed with practical recommendations to help clients strengthen their operations. * **Team Collaboration:** Collaborate with colleagues across different disciplines—like tax, corporate finance and company secretarial—to ensure a smooth and efficient audit process. * **Communication Pro:** Keep your partner in the loop, flagging significant issues and progress on your assignments. You will work closely with partners, directors and other managers to deliver top\-notch services to our clients. * **Quality and Compliance:** Deliver audits that meet the highest standards, addressing key risks and sticking to deadlines and budgets. **How we are going to support your development:** * Crowe will fully sponsor your accounting qualification: You will have the freedom to choose which accounting qualification you will pursue. ACA? ACCA? It’s your pick! * We will support your studies by providing generous study leave so you can dedicate time to pass your exams. * Flexibility to attend your classes and tuitions. * You will work closely with our senior team and will be mentored and supported to achieve your personal and professional goals. You will gain experience and develop competencies in practice dealing with clients and carrying out meaningful and relevant work * **What We’re Looking For:** * **Academic Performance** : You have achieved or are on track to achieve a 2\.2 or higher in your undergraduate degree and have recently graduated or will graduate in 2026\. * **Future Accountants:** You are excited about a career in accounting, even if you come from an academic background that is not accounting. * **Eligibility:** You have the right to work full time in the Republic of Ireland. * **Communication Champs:** You have strong verbal and written communication skills, with an eye for detail and a knack for problem\-solving. * **Attention to Detail:** You are a master of the small stuff and know that details matter. **Our offer:** * Alongside a fully sponsored accounting qualification, we offer a competitive reward package that comprises salary, benefits, development opportunities and wellbeing programmes. Some of them are: + 22 days of annual leave + Pension with 100% employer matching contributions + Tax saver travel pass + Cycle to Work Scheme + And more… * Work\-life balance: our team enjoy compressed working weeks during the summer and hybrid work options. Accelerate career progression through ongoing support and investment in your development. * **Why Join Us?** Because we believe in nurturing talent from day one. You will get mentorship from experienced professionals, access to ongoing learning and development opportunities, and the chance to make a real impact in a supportive, inclusive environment. Plus, we know how to have fun—whether it is team social events, community events, or just celebrating a job well done! Ready to write a story that matters? Apply now and start your journey with Crowe! **Equal opportunity** Crowe is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and anyone seeking employment at Crowe is considered based on merit, qualifications, competence and talent. We don’t regard colour, religion, race, national origin, sexual orientation, ancestry, citizenship, sex, marital or family status, disability, gender, or any other legally protected status when making our hiring decisions. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know.

title: Audit and Supplier Qualification Specialist
description: Life changing therapies. Global impact. Bridge to thousands of biopharma companies and their patients. **We are PCI.** Our investment is in People who make an impact, drive progress and create a better tomorrow. Our strategy includes building teams across our global network to pioneer and shape the future of PCI. **Summary of Role** The role is to implement and execute tasks relating to GMP auditing and supplier qualification in the PCI sites in Ireland. **Key Responsibilities** * Support, participate and host both Client and Regulatory audits. * Management of audit responses and ensure closure of actions within agreed timeframes. * On\-going interaction with clients in relation to audits to ensure expectations and timelines are communicated in a proactive and timely manner * Management and co\-ordinate all aspects on site Supplier Management Programme, including supplier audits, preparation of QTA’s and supplier qualification. * Ensure on\-going compliance with customer, regulatory and internal requirements. * Ensure safe work practices are being followed at all times, report any near misses immediately to the EHS Department. * Raise Deviations, Corrective Action Requests, and Non\-Conformance reports as required in a timely manner and ensure that issues are communicated to the auditing Manager/designee * Generation of SOP/Forms/JA's/WI's on MasterControl * Ensure safe work practices are being followed at all times, report any defects immediately to the Safety Officer and attend Health \& Safety meetings. * Interact and communicate daily with staff and support departments. * To undertake such tasks and to manage specific and/or ad hoc projects as and when required to meet department and business needs and within the scope of the job\-holder’s capabilities. * Carry out various other additional tasks that may be required from time to time in accordance with the overall goal of the company **Knowledge / Skills \& Experience** **Essential:** * Bachelor’s or associate degree or Diploma / Certificate in Pharmaceutical and/or life science. (e.g. quality assurance, , or similar). May substitute experience in lieu of educational requirements. * 3\-5 years previous supplier management and auditing experience is preferred with particular emphasis on hosting and conducting audits. * Lead auditor qualifications would be advantageous. * Good working knowledge of cGMP and associated regulations (Eudralex Volume 4; ISO 13485\). * Experience in a similar role / working in Quality Assurance, Quality Systems, Compliance * To have demonstrable experience within Quality Systems. * Capability to follow clear goals, communicate clearly and ensure progress to completion meeting predefined targets * Ability to manage multiple tasks and set priorities. * Good interpersonal skills. * Good Written communication skills \#LI\-JP1 Join us and be part of building the bridge between life changing therapies and patients. title: BIM Modeller
description: BIM Modeller, Leixlip, Co. Kildare Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centers, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centers, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Responsible for production of 3D Construction models from base information – Base Design, PID’s, Scan \& point cloud data * Provision of construction detailing on the systems to enable the automatic production of isometrics and BOM * Clash management on all systems * Design and detailing of services support systems * Adherence to Mercury CAD and BIM manuals and processes * Collaboration with other trades * Field surveying and verification * Management of drawing schedules * Development of models and reports in line with scheduling requirements * Problem solving and resolution of co\-ordination and design road blocks * Production of GA’s and detailing sheets * Attendance at design and construction reviews * Assistance in the overall development of the BIM protocols and systems * Work with the BIM steering committee in driving and implementing improvement roadmap plans * Implement, lead and drive the new programs Mercury are putting in place to ensure Mercury Group BIM is recognised as an industry leader for BIM capability and innovation. * Travel to sites (both in Ireland and abroad) may be required. * Networking and an ability to work well in teams is a requirement * Training and mentoring of new entry staff on completion of training modules * Liaison with Mercury site staff in interpreting and amending models * Ensure integration of models into federated model for overall co\-ordination * Development of 3D coordinated services and structure layouts for clash control * Remain fully up to date with the drawing management * Development of mechanical \& electrical systems and designs, coordinated building services layouts, management of drawing registers and standards. * Technical report writing * The production of title: Barista
description: ☕ **Barista – Join Our Team and Brew Up a Great Career with Us!** **The Role:** This isn’t just another job — it’s your chance to **kickstart a career** in a brand that values **growth, development, and promoting from within.** As a Barista, you’ll be part of a supportive team where learning is part of every day and your potential truly matters. ✨ **Why Join Us?** * Full training provided — no experience needed! * A clear path to progress — many of our leaders started as Baristas * Ongoing support to help you grow in confidence and skills * A fun, energetic environment where every shift brings something new 🎁 **What We Offer:** * Complimentary handcrafted coffee on every shift * Employee discounts across all Costa locations * Full training and ongoing development opportunities * A friendly, inclusive, and supportive team culture * A workplace where you can grow, learn, and be yourself 📍 **Make it Yours:** This role is based in Costa Coffee Celbridge. 💬 **Apply today and bring your love for coffee to life with Costa!**

title: Bookkeeper
description: Job description Are you detail\-oriented, organised, and experienced in bookkeeping? We are looking for a reliable and efficient **Bookkeeper** to join our team. If you have experience in managing financial records and want to work in a dynamic environment, this could be the perfect role for you! **About the Role** As a **Bookkeeper**, you will be responsible for maintaining accurate financial records, handling invoicing, payroll, and other accounting\-related tasks. You will work closely with the finance team to ensure the smooth operation of all financial processes. **Key Responsibilities** * Maintain and update financial records, including ledgers, invoices, and receipts. * Assist in payroll preparation and ensure accurate tax filings. * Handle VAT calculations and filings. * Process payments, reconcile bank statements, and monitor cash flow. * Prepare financial reports and summaries on a monthly and yearly basis. * Manage accounts payable and receivable. * Assist in payroll preparation and ensure accurate tax filings. * Collaborate with the finance team to ensure compliance with accounting standards and legal requirements. * Assist in preparing documents for audits and liaising with external accountants when needed. **Who We Are Looking For** * **Experience**: At least 3 years of experience in bookkeeping or a similar finance role. * **Payroll Experience:** Hands\-on experience with payroll processing. * **VAT Knowledge:** Understanding of VAT regulations and filing requirements. * **Qualifications**: A relevant qualification in accounting, finance, or bookkeeping is preferred. * **Tech\-savvy**: Proficiency with accounting software (e.g. Xero) and Microsoft Excel. * **Attention to Detail**: You’re thorough, precise, and committed to accuracy. * **Organized \& Efficient**: Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage time effectively. * **Integrity**: Trustworthy and able to handle sensitive financial data confidentially. * **Communication**: Good written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to work well in a team. **What We Offer** * Opportunities for professional development and career growth. * 20 days of annual leave. If you are dedicated to contributing to urban development, have a keen eye for detail, and possess strong bookkeeping skills, we want to hear from you! Join us at Speedy Flower and be part of a team committed to excellence in construction and city development. **To apply, please send your CV to hr@speedyflower.net** We look forward to welcoming you to our team! Job Type: Full\-time Pay: From €16\.00 per hour Benefits: * Company events Work Location: In person

title: Business Development Representative
description: **Business Development Representative (German Speaking – Dublin)** About Your New Employer – SaaS Software * Join a world\-leading tech platform with a renowned reputation for innovative solutions in professional development. * Become part of a collaborative, Dublin\-based team that values mentorship, learning, and public recognition of achievements. * Experience a vibrant office atmosphere, including regular social and team engagement activities. About Your New Job \- DACH BDR * As a German\-speaking Business Development Representative (BDR) for the DACH region, you will generate qualified leads through outbound calls, LinkedIn outreach, and email campaigns. * Collaborate with enterprise account teams and work closely with both Account Executives and Customer Success Managers. * Report to a Dublin\-based team leader and benefit from structured mentorship, coaching, and exposure to the full sales cycle. * Your goal: create 15 qualified meetings per month through targeted outbound activity and strategic market segmentation. * Clear progression path: high performers move to Senior BDR within 6 months and onward to Account Executive or enterprise roles within 12 months. What Skills You Need * Native/fluent level German, written and spoken * Ideally 1 year’s sales experience in a telesales evironment * Driven to learn, comfortable with cold calling and all outbound activity (phone, LinkedIn, email). * Based in Dublin and available onsite every Wednesday and Thursday. * Willing and able to engage prospects by phone. What’s on Offer * €45,000 base salary \+ €30,000 variable commission (Total OTE: €75,000\). * Unlimited paid time off (PTO). * Full benefits package: private health, dental, pension match. * Free access to a premium learning platform and MEDDPIC certification training. * Accelerated career development and mentorship from experienced commercial and enterprise sales professionals.

title: Buyer
description: **Buyer – Retail \- Hybrid in Dublin 24 (3 days in office)** This is not a traditional, desk‑bound buying role focused on spreadsheets and reports. We’re partnering with a large, fast‑moving Irish retail group (300\+ stores) that is transforming how it buys, ranges and launches branded products across its estate. As part of this evolution, they’re hiring a commercially driven Buyer to take full ownership of a Front of Shop category, from supplier negotiation through to in‑store execution. **What you’ll own** * Full commercial ownership of your category * Direct negotiation with global and branded suppliers * Control over promotions, space, launches and margin * Accountability for delivering the commercial number **What you’ll be doing** * Owning the end‑to‑end commercial cycle for assigned categories * Negotiating trading terms with established global suppliers * Driving promotional plans, JBPs, margin and sell‑through * Securing exclusive launches, GWP activity and first‑to‑market deals * Managing planograms, space allocation and range performance * Representing your category in weekly trade and commercial meetings **What you’ll need** * 2\+ years’ experience in Buying or Assistant Buying * Experience with branded, supplier‑led categories (e.g. FMCG, Confectionery, Beauty, Health, Electrical, Power Tools, etc.) * Confidence owning and delivering against a P\&L * Comfortable working independently in a lean, hands‑on environment **This opportunity is a full\-time permanent role, based in Dublin 24, hybrid (3 days onsite).** **To hear more about this position or to discuss your suitability, apply today and we will be in touch with more information.** **By applying here you agree to RECRUITERS storing your CV in our secure applicant tracking system (ATS; Bullhorn). As your consultants, we are dedicated to helping you find a new job in your field of expertise and, equally, to keeping your personal information secure at all times. Your CV will not be sent to any third party without your consent.**

title: CRM Specialist
description: **Who We Are** At 2K, we create some of the most iconic and culture\-shaping video games in entertainment, including ***NBA® 2K*** , one of the top\-selling franchises in the world, and legendary titles like ***BioShock®*** *,* ***Borderlands®*** *,* ***Mafia*** *,* ***Sid Meier’s Civilization*** **®** , and ***XCOM®*** , as well as fan favorites ***WWE® 2K*** *,* ***TopSpin®*** , and ***PGA TOUR® 2K*** *.* We build unforgettable experiences by pushing the boundaries of creativity, authenticity and innovation across every genre. Our portfolio is brought to life by some of the most influential game development studios in the world. **Visual Concepts, Firaxis Games, Hangar 13, Cat Daddy Games, 31st Union, Cloud Chamber, Gearbox, HB Studios,** and **2K SportsLab** create world\-class experiences across platforms. But what truly powers 2K is our people. We believe the best ideas come from teams that feel empowered, supported, and inspired. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive workplace where people are encouraged to **come as they are** and do their best work. **What We Need** The CRM Specialist is an established professional contributor responsible for spearheading CRM activations—including email, push, and lifecycle campaigns—for industry\-leading titles across 2K’s global portfolio. This role manages the end\-to\-end campaign process, from inception and planning to execution, optimization, and performance analysis. Residing within the Player Connections department, the CRM Specialist works closely with cross\-functional teams to deepen player engagement and ensure the commercial success of 2K’s out\-of\-game ecosystem. **What You’ll Do** * Campaign Execution: Owns the end\-to\-end CRM campaign creation process, ensuring flawless delivery of activations and automation workflows to millions of global players. * Strategic Optimization: Leads efficiency improvements for CRM workflows; identifies performance trends through rigorous data analysis to drive business decisions. * Stakeholder Collaboration: Partners with Brand, Product Marketing, Analytics, and Legal teams; takes confidence in leading cross\-functional syncs, driving structured discussions, and defining clear next steps. * Creative Partnership: Collaborates with creative teams to develop and edit responsive email templates that perform across all devices and platforms. * Marketing Technology: Works with the MarTech team on audience creation, data architecture, opt\-in flows, and CRM setup for new IP launches. * Project \& Time Management: Effectively manages multiple priorities and maintains structured workflows to deliver high\-impact campaigns within tight timelines. * Reporting \& Insights: Provides regular updates and performance reporting to stakeholders, sharing key learnings across business units to ensure state\-of\-the\-art publishing. * Guidance \& Mentorship: Coaches and guides graduates rotating through the team, managing their daily workflows and performance reviews. **Core Competencies** * Adaptability: A self\-starter who fearlessly tackles new challenges and brings a fresh perspective to existing processes. * Communication \& Leadership: Demonstrates excellent relationship management skills with the confidence to lead cross\-functional meetings and drive alignment. * Attention to Detail: Maintains meticulous standards to ensure "pixel\-perfect" execution across high\-volume global campaigns. * Organization: Highly organized with a proven ability to prioritize tasks and manage complex workflows in a fast\-paced environment. * Problem Solving: Develops solutions to tactical business issues with clear objectives and recommends enhancements to improve system effectiveness. **What Will Make You A Great Fit** * Experience: Previous professional experience in CRM, digital marketing, or a related field. * Technical Knowledge: Strong understanding of automation workflows and email template development and editing. * Analytical Skills: Proven ability in data analysis, creating reports for stakeholders, and identifying trends to drive data\-driven business decisions. * Operational Knowledge: Established understanding of CRM tools, email service providers (ESPs), and audience segmentation. * Time Management: Strong time management skills with a demonstrated ability to meet strict deadlines while managing multiple projects. **Preferred Qualifications** * AI Literate: Familiar with AI processes, workflows and general adoption especially as relating to Email build, QA \& reporting. * Agency Experience: Experience working with Email agency providers across a range of Email related workstreams. * Industry Savvy: Familiarity with popular 2K titles and the gaming industry landscape. * Mentorship Experience: Experience guiding junior staff or entry\-level team members on workflows and performance. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to ensuring that qualified individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform their essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us if you need reasonable accommodation. ***Please note that 2K Games and its studios never uses instant messaging apps or personal email accounts to contact prospective employees or conduct interviews and when emailing, only use 2K.com accounts.***

title: Chemistry Specialist (Masters/PhDs)
description: **Chemistry Specialist (Masters/PhDs) \- AI Training** **About The Role** What if your years of chemistry expertise could directly shape how AI reasons through the most complex scientific problems in the world? We're looking for Chemistry Specialists with advanced degrees to design, solve, and evaluate graduate\-level chemistry problems that train cutting\-edge AI models to think like expert scientists. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role built for working chemists and researchers who want to apply their deep domain knowledge in a high\-impact, forward\-looking way. No AI background required — just serious chemistry expertise and a commitment to scientific rigor. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Design and solve complex, real\-world chemistry problems spanning organic, inorganic, physical, or computational chemistry * Review and evaluate AI\-generated chemistry content for scientific accuracy, depth, and clarity * Craft challenging problem statements that push AI reasoning to graduate\-level standards * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and fellow domain experts to improve model performance * Ensure all deliverables meet high standards of scientific rigor and precision * Work independently on task\-based assignments — fully on your own schedule **Who You Are** * Master's or PhD in Chemistry or a closely related field * Deep expertise in at least one of: organic, inorganic, physical, or computational chemistry * Comfortable working with technical concepts at a graduate or research level * Naturally detail\-oriented with strong written communication skills in English * Self\-motivated and reliable when working independently * Experience coding in Python or using computational chemistry tools (e.g., Gaussian, VASP) is a strong plus **Nice to Have** * Prior experience with data annotation, content evaluation, or quality assurance workflows * Familiarity with AI tools or language model evaluation as an end user * Background in academic research, scientific publishing, or curriculum development * Experience designing problem sets, exams, or instructional materials at a graduate level **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects alongside the world's leading research labs * Fully remote and flexible — work when and where it suits you * Apply your advanced chemistry knowledge in a meaningful, intellectually stimulating way * Freelance autonomy with the structure of impactful, task\-based work * Contribute to AI systems that will shape how science is understood and communicated at scale * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Cleaner - Greenhills
description: **Position Overview:** As a Cleaner at Boylesports, you will play a key role in maintaining a clean, organized, and welcoming environment for our customers and staff. You will be responsible for ensuring cleanliness across various areas of our premises, including bathrooms, betting areas, and common spaces. **Key Responsibilities:** * Perform general cleaning duties such as sweeping, mopping, vacuuming, dusting, and sanitizing surfaces. * Clean and disinfect bathrooms, including sinks, toilets, and floors. * Empty bins and replace liners. * Maintain cleanliness of betting terminals and gaming areas. * Monitor and replenish cleaning supplies. * Follow established cleaning schedules and procedures. * Adhere to health and safety guidelines and protocols. * Report any maintenance issues or safety hazards to management promptly. **Qualifications and Skills:** * Previous experience in commercial cleaning or housekeeping is preferred but not essential. * Must live within a commutable distance from the store. * Ability to work independently and efficiently. * Strong attention to detail and thoroughness in completing tasks. * Good communication skills and a positive attitude. * Ability to follow instructions. **Working Schedule:**The work schedule is 4\-7 days per week, depending on the store. The shifts are 1\-2 hours per day, outside of Boylesports trading hours. **Join Our Team:**If you are dedicated to maintaining cleanliness and take pride in ensuring a tidy environment, we encourage you to apply for this position. Life assurance is provided and joining our pension scheme is optional. Apply today and become part of our team!

title: Client Advisor - Dublin (4 days)
description: **Job Description** At Tiffany \& Co., joy is central to everything we do, from crafting our exceptional pieces to inspiring clients to express and celebrate the many facets of love. It’s a skill that we’ve been perfecting since 1837, one empowered by our daring vision and entrepreneurial spirit. Together, each generation of employees honor our past while dreaming of our future. We are looking for thoughtful, curious, and optimistic talents, seeking to deliver excellence and to contribute to Tiffany \& Co.’s continued legacy. As Tiffany Client Advisors, we are dedicated to welcome all in the Tiffany world. We achieve our individual \& store sales goals by inspiring our clients to dream and always finding a way to celebrate them. Together with our clients, we build long\-lasting personal connections. **Thoughtful** * Deliver unparalleled service and results that uphold standards of excellence and luxury etiquette * Demonstrate an entrepreneurial mindset to exceed sales objectives and KPIs * Be a trusted Advisor, building client development strategy to drive sales, cross\-sales and client loyalty, curating memorable experiences through sales of new creations and services * Ensure compliance with Tiffany and LVMH procedures, uphold operational excellence standards (inventory, reservation management, transfers, care services excellence, etc.) * Contribute to an inclusive and supportive team environment, centered on the belief that People Make the Difference **Curious** * Inspire clients through authentic brand storytelling, effectively conveying Tiffany’s legacy of craftsmanship, brand commitment and integrity * Connect with clients and team members by asking strategic questions and establishing lasting relationships * Think and act with intention to elevate, surprise and celebrate clients' special moments and build trust through proactive and relevant services (including personalization and product care services) * Display professionalism and exercise agility, adapting behavior and implementing feedback, appropriately **Optimistic** * Exercise resilience through new challenging assignments and celebrate innovation when adopting new ways of working * Exhibit a positive, ambitious and collaborative attitude, instilling an inclusive team culture of Joy **Your Profile** * Minimum 3 years of experience in luxury retail environment or client\-related experience (e.g., hospitality) * Experienced sales and clienteling professional in an omnichannel luxury environment * Proven track record in achieving sales results and cultivating relationships with a diverse client base * Passion for luxury retail environments; jewelry / watch expertise is a plus * Collaborative team player with strong interpersonal and communication skills * Thoughtful and service\-oriented with knowledge of luxury etiquette * Experienced in deploying sales strategies and clienteling initiatives * Digitally\-savvy and at ease with omnichannel tools * Proficiency with Point of Sales (POS) systems, client tracking systems and Microsoft Outlook/email. * Flexibility to work non\-traditional hours, including days, nights, weekends, and holidays. * Must have authorization to work in the United States or in the country where the position is based. **Preferred** * Proficiency in multiple languages * A college/university degree * Graduate Gemologist degree or previous Gemological Institute of America (GIA) course work *Tiffany \& Co. is formed from many facets \- talented people who make our community stronger with their creativity, perspective, and lived experience. This is why diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging (DEIB) are at the core of our business and our culture. We’re committed to creating a more inclusive jewelry industry that empowers people of all backgrounds to shine and evolve to new heights.* *We believe reaching your full potential requires a solid foundation, and your well\-being is a central pillar. Tiffany employees are eligible for comprehensive benefits, including global benefits like adoption and surrogacy assistance, and parental leave, as well as programs that support mental, emotional, physical, and financial well\-being. We also offer competitive pay, medical, dental, and eyecare, pension plans, paid time off, alongside other meaningful employee offerings.*
