title: Tooling Apprentice
description: **Role Purpose:** The tooling apprentice will support the everyday functions of the toolmakers within the Tooling Departments in a high\-volume injection moulding manufacturing setting. **Key Responsibilities:** The tooling apprentice will: * Compile standard specifications/procedures/records for each mould and monitor all existing moulds to standard specifications * Assist the toolmakers whilst they fit and assemble parts to make, repair, or modify dies, jigs, gauges, and tools, using machine tools and hand tools * Support the toolmakers with tool builds * Support initial tool trials and validations * Support tool changes where required * Carry out essential cleaning on parts, slides, tools * Support all aspects of machine and equipment maintenance, installation and modification * Document all work carried out, including upgrades/repairs on existing equipment/tools * Maintain the highest standards of hygiene and housekeeping * Ensure highest Quality Compliance standards, participating and complying with the Quality Management System * Identify and generate opportunities for improvement and actively participate in the implementation of corrective action plans and continuous improvement initiatives * Comply with all company policies and procedures, including quality requirements, financial controls, health and safety regulations, etc. *The above list of duties and responsibilities is not exhaustive, and the role holder may be required to carry out other duties which may reasonably be assigned by management*. **The ideal candidate:** It is essential that the tooling apprentice has a keen interest in the tooling department. A good knowledge of injection moulding and how tooling is important in the process, an understanding of how to trouble shoot and experience with repairs and maintenance would be an advantage. Possessing excellent interpersonal, teamwork, IT and communication skills, it is essential that the tooling apprentice works effectively with others to achieve challenging objectives. The tooling apprentice will be a self\-starter, organized, result\-oriented with a strong work ethic and the ability and resilience to work under pressure. ***MGS (Ireland) is an equal opportunities employer.***

title: Town Planning Professional – CSR (Dublin & Cork)
description: #### **Opportunity for Experienced Planning Professionals** CSR is an established, award\-winning multi‑disciplinary consultancy delivering high‑quality planning, design and construction services across the built environment. Driven by a strong commitment to excellence, the practice continues to grow its portfolio of public and private sector clients. We are currently seeking Town Planning Professionals to join our Dublin or Cork offices. The role offers exposure to a broad range of projects, including large‑scale residential schemes, commercial and retail development, urban regeneration, tourism, masterplanning, infrastructure and environmental initiatives. The successful candidate will work on varied and rewarding projects within a collaborative, multi‑disciplinary team, with clear opportunities for professional development and role progression aligned to their skills and experience. ‍**Role Responsibilities:** * Managing projects end to end, working closely with clients, stakeholders and internal teams. * Developing project plans, overseeing budgets and ensuring the timely delivery of high‑quality services. * Ensuring efficient delivery of client instructions. * Coordinating with statutory authorities, including County Councils and An Bord Pleanála. * Designing and implementing strategic responses to new legislation and policy changes. * Acting as a company representative at client meetings and across social media to support business development. **Ideal Candidate:** * Professionals with 5\+ years planning and development experience. * Accredited planning qualification with IPI and/or RTPI membership, or equivalent. * Candidates should have 5\+ years of planning and development experience. * They should be strong communicators with excellent report\-writing skills, commercial awareness, the ability to work independently and collaboratively, and proven leadership skills. * Expert knowledge of Irish planning legislation and regulations. * Proven expertise in development planning, feasibility, environmental planning and planning applications, appeals and reports. * Strong analytical and decision\-making skills in complex planning matters. * Strong communicator and collaborative team member with a client\-focused approach. * Support junior team members and collaborate with our design, master\-planning and GIS teams * Excellent written, verbal, presentation and organisational skills. **Salary and Benefits:** Salary is negotiable and will be commensurate with skills and experience. CSR supports Professional Membership and Continuing Professional Development (CPD) as essential aspects of this role. Our range of benefits is tailored to meet individual needs. CSR is dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace, offering flexible arrangements and supporting a healthy work\-life balance. This is a full\-time position based in our Dublin or Cork office. Candidate must be eligible to work in Ireland. ‍ **How to Apply:** Candidates interested in the position should apply by emailing an up\-to\-date Curriculum Vitae and Cover Letter/Email to **info@csrlandplan.ie**

title: Trade Counter Sales, Dublin 1
description: Rexel/Kellihers Electrical is a worldwide expert in distributing electric and energy\-efficient products and services. Our goal is to provide smart electrical solutions that improve the comfort, safety and security of our customers’ homes, businesses, and industries. We are currently recruiting a Trade Counter Sales person for our Croke Park branch in Dublin 1\. You’ll join a busy, supportive team. The role is customer\-focused and ideal for someone who thrives on helping professionals find the right products quickly and accurately. **Job Description** Role Purpose: To provide product knowledge of electrical products along with an excellent customer service. Responsibilities: * Respond to sales enquiries either by phone or over the counter, provide quotations and ensure maximum level of sales. * Offer alternative products, up\-sell where possible, promote own brands and cross brand sales. * Taking orders and preparing them. * Purchasing from MRP within the branch. * Involvement in all aspects of the business on site. * Other ad hoc duties. **Qualifications** Experience: * Sales experience essential. * Electrical Wholesale experience and electrical product knowledge is an advantage. * Experience with operating a forklift is an advantage. Technical / Functional skills: * Must be able to demonstrate good organisation and time management skills. * Good attention to detail and be able to prioritize and work well under pressure. * Strong communication skills to deal on a regular basis with colleagues and customers. * Good IT skills ( Word, Outlook, Excel). * Fluency in English both written and spoken. **Additional Information** Benefits: * Bike to Work Scheme * Company Events * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Private medical insurance * Sick pay * Store discount At Rexel we welcome people from all backgrounds with diverse perspectives and experiences. Our goal is to create a workplace where everyone feels respected and empowered to succeed as we understand that our success and innovation is enhanced by an inclusive and diverse workforce.

title: Trainee Accountant
description: We are seeking a trainee accountant to join a growing finance team, based in Dublin City Centre. This role represents an excellent entry point for an accounting graduate or someone early in their career seeking to build a strong and well\-rounded foundation in finance. The position offers broad exposure across core accounting functions, and the opportunity to develop both technical and commercial skills within a professional finance environment. **Responsibilities:** * Process supplier invoices and support the accounts payable function. * Maintaining rent schedules for residential properties. * Prepare and maintain accurate bank and account reconciliations. * Weekly payroll operations. **Requirements:** * 1 year’s experience in an accounting or finance support role. * Strong understanding of accounts payable, receivable, and bank reconciliations. * Strong attention to detail, accuracy, and organisational skills. * Proficient in Microsoft Excel and accounting software (Sage or Yardi experience an advantage). This role offers great hands\-on experience within a collaborative finance team, providing clear scope for professional growth, skills development, and long\-term career progression. For more information, apply directly or contact Matthew Fleming at mfleming@elevatepartners.ie.

title: Trainee Kitchen and Bedroom Furniture Fitter
description: BeSpace is seeking to hire a skilled Trainee Kitchen and Wardrobe Fitters to join our growing team. Due to increasing demand, we are looking for professionals who take pride in delivering outstanding installation services and consistently high\-quality results. You will be responsible for fitting kitchens and wardrobes to exact specifications, ensuring every project meets our quality standards and exceeds client expectations. **Responsibilities** · Interpret and work from technical drawings and AutoCAD plans with accuracy. · Install kitchens and wardrobes to the highest standard in clients’ homes · Report and escalate any delivery or product issues promptly. · Ensure all installations comply with BeSpace quality standards, striving to exceed customer expectations. · Maintain a clean, safe, and professional working environment on site. **Requirements:** * 1\- 2 years’ experience fitting kitchens and wardrobes. * Proven ability to meet both quality and productivity performance standards. * Strong attention to detail and commitment to precision workmanship. * Excellent problem\-solving skills and ability to work independently. * Valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling certification. **We Offer :** * Competitive salary and benefits package. * Opportunities for professional growth and development. * A supportive, collaborative work environment. * The chance to be part of a growing company with a reputation for quality and excellence. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €45,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay * Store discount * Wellness program Application question(s): * Are you available to work full time? Experience: * Fitting: 2 years (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: UI Engineer
description: We’re partnering with a well\-funded early\-stage startup building an AI\-powered operational intelligence platform for the food manufacturing industry. The team is tackling complex forecasting, production planning, and supply chain optimization problems through advanced predictive analytics and modern AI systems. They’re now hiring a Founding Design Engineer to own both product experience and frontend development for a highly interactive B2B platform. What You’ll Do * Lead end\-to\-end UX and frontend development * Build intuitive interfaces for complex forecasting and operational data * Design AI\-native workflows and chatbot experiences * Create interactive dashboards and data visualizations * Collaborate closely with product, research, and engineering teams * Drive rapid iteration using product analytics and user feedback Requirements * Strong experience designing B2B SaaS products * Expert in Figma, React, Next.js, TypeScript, and TailwindCSS * Experience visualizing complex datasets and time\-series data * Familiarity with AI/LLM products and streaming AI interfaces * Startup mindset with strong ownership and execution skills Additional Info * Competitive salary \+ meaningful equity * 35 days paid leave \+ strong benefits package * No visa sponsorship available * Must be authorized to work in Ireland

title: Warehouse Operative
description: **Warehouse Operative \- Ashbourne** Choice Stores, a rapidly growing retail company, is searching for dedicated and hardworking Warehouse Operatives to join our amazing team. If you're passionate about logistics and looking to take your career to the next level, we want to hear from you! Responsibilities: * Accurately pick, pack, and process customer orders in a timely manner * Load and unload delivery vehicles, adhering to safe lifting techniques and guidelines * Maintain stock inventory, ensuring accuracy and organization * Ability to build solid safe pallets * Ensure all deliveries are checked in and out accurately from the warehouse * Ensure that Goods Receiving and Dispatching procedures are followed at all times * Operate warehouse equipment, including forklifts and pallet jacks, in a safe and efficient manner * Participate in routine stock checks and report discrepancies * Work closely with team members to achieve warehouse targets and goals * Comply with all company policies and procedures, including health and safety regulations Requirements: * Previous experience in a warehouse or similar role is preferred * Forklift certification is an advantage, but not essential as training will be provided * Excellent organizational and time management skills * Strong attention to detail and accuracy * Ability to work well in a fast\-paced, team\-oriented environment * Physically fit and able to lift and move heavy items safely * Positive attitude and strong work ethic Why join Choice Stores? * Competitive salary and benefits package * flexible working hours * Opportunities for growth and career advancement * Supportive and inclusive work environment * Comprehensive training and development programs Don't miss out on this fantastic opportunity to join the Choice Stores family! Apply now and take your career to new heights as a Warehouse Operative.

title: part-time Primary Care Nurse
description: **40,000\.00 \~ 50,000\.00 EUR annually** Location Usher's Island Dublin Ireland*This job ends on 14 June 2026* **About Us:** At Dublin Simon Community, we’re dedicated to making home a reality. We empower individuals to access and sustain housing through services like prevention, addiction treatment, emergency response, and targeted interventions, in collaboration with our partners. If you share our values and want to make a difference, we’d love to hear from you. We’re looking for client\-focused, friendly, and adaptable individuals to join our team. We prioritise best practices in HR, including staff training, communication, and involvement in decision\-making, to create a supportive and effective work environment. **About the Role** **Contract Type****:** Permanent, Part\-time **Working Hours****:** 15 hours a week, Monday\-Friday, 5\-day liability.**Salary****:** 40,000\-50,000 pro\-rata DOE **Location:** moving between Emergency Services (Harcourt Street, Maple House, Longfields, Carmans hall) and Long Term Accommodation (Chester House, Oak House and Riversdale) depending on needs of the service. We are seeking a dedicated and compassionate Primary Care Nurse to join our team! This unique role offers the opportunity to make a profound impact on the lives of homeless adults, many of whom may be in active addiction or experience complex mental or physical health concerns. The team provide on\-site nursing care to people experiencing homelessness to improve their quality of life and refer them to external medical or mental health services. The aim of primary care, is to reduce barriers to healthcare for homeless clients through on\-site nursing intervention, advocacy and liaising with external services. As an advocate for some of the most vulnerable members of our community, you will be at the forefront of delivering essential healthcare services, supporting patients through their recovery journeys, and helping to reduce barriers to healthcare for homeless clients. You will be part of a comprehensive nursing team that is dedicated to improving health outcomes for people experiencing homelessness. You will work closely with your primary care team and the wider inclusion health community, liaising with external services such as; SafetyNet, MQI \& hospital\-based inclusion health teams. **Role Responsibilities** * To work with the Primary Care Nurse Manager to plan the health care of residents, address gaps and blocks to client care and respond to needs of service accordingly. * Conduct basic nursing assessments with clients to establish their health care needs and link them in to relevant hospital/community\-based teams. * Develop individual nursing care plans for clients and work closely and with teams to deliver outcomes and ensure continuity of care. * Provide on\-site nursing care in homeless accommodation: routine observations, attend emergencies, medication education and monitoring chronic health issues. * Collaboration of care between healthcare providers. * Support and empower clients in their recovery journey and adopt a harm reduction approach to care. * Provide comprehensive, effective and efficient coordination of healthcare to service users accessing Emergency Services/Long Term Accommodation. * Maintain a caseload as agreed with Nurse Manager in line with service needs. * Work as part of the primary care nursing team, attending weekly case conferences and handing over relevant health/social care information daily. * Attend bi\-weekly MDT with external social inclusion agencies and handover care of clients. * Attend and contribute to weekly case reviews at each service and address any follow up care that is needed. * Deliver training to staff/clients as indicated by service need and directed by nurse manager eg sexual health awareness etc. * On\-site wound care provided by nursing team for clients; further training provided by Tissue Viability Nurse. * Delivery of the Needle and Syringe Exchange Programme and safer injecting advice to service users and comply with all clinical governance requirements. * Respond to overdoses in services – naloxone training provided. * Complete all administrative tasks such as report writing, data collection, maintenance of accurate nursing and medical records, up to date client case notes. * Work closely with support staff and key workers to ensure the health care needs (mental health and general health) of clients are met and that the continuity of care is of an optimum standard. * To liaise with and develop relationships with key stakeholders and establish and expand on the services available to clients including referrals to; Public Health Nurses, Physiotherapists, GP’s , Occupational Therapists, Addiction Treatment Services, Mental Health Teams, Hospices etc. * Conduct external health service visitations to meet with client and MDT for continuity of care. This can involve arranging MDT meetings, case conferences and discharge planning meetings. * Advocate for clients with external services, ensuring the clients best interest at the forefront of all conversations. * Contribute and support the Primary Care Nurse Manager in the development of health promotion initiatives as needed across Dublin Simon services * Support key workers to manage the healthcare needs of a complex caseload of clients presenting with; mental health, physical health and substance misuse issues. * To participate in Governance for Quality and Safety initiatives and participating in own professional development. * Work within and assist development, implementation and review of policies and procedures in order to ensure quality standards are adhered to in services. * Work within scope of practice. **Qualifications and Experience** **Essential for the role:** * Registered Nurse with NMBI. * Evidence of PIN renewal with NMBI for current year. * Willingness to work with people experiencing homelessness and addiction. * Minimum 2 years post graduate experience. **Desirable:*** Experience working with marginalised groups. * Experience in addiction / mental health / infectious diseases. * Further education in addiction studies / trauma awareness / wound care. **Benefits to working with Dublin Simon Community** * 25 days of annual leave * 5% matched contribution to your pension * Paid Sick Leave Policy * Paid Maternity Leave Policy * Bike to Work Scheme * Commuter Travel Tax Savers Ticket * 1st\-week Comprehensive Training * Progression Opportunities **For further information on the role please contact CNM2, Ciara Cotter ciaracotter@dubsimon.ie** **Please note, if you are unsure of any of the requirements, contact the Recruitment Team on (01\) 6354860** ***Please note that we reserve the right to offer the position to a successful candidate prior to the deadline of receipt of applications and we advise that you apply early.*** **Please note:** If you are unsure of any of the requirements, please contact the Recruitment Team at (01\) 6354860\. Shortlisting will continue throughout the recruitment process. Dublin Simon Community is an equal opportunities employer and encourage applications from all qualified individuals.
